items.title: Govt rolls out RM60bil drive to strengthen micro, small and medium enterprises
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government has planned more than 300 development programmes worth RM60 billion this year to help Malaysia's micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) boost growth. Sim said micro, small and medium enterprises remain the backbone of Malaysia's economy despite global challenges, including rising operating costs driven by supply chain disruptions and geopolitical uncertainty. He said government support for micro, small and medium enterprises had yielded positive results last year, with 344 entrepreneurship and development programmes worth RM31.63 billion helping to create 6,600 new entrepreneurs.

items.title: BN confident of winning back Bukit Kepong seat as Johor election campaign begin
items.summary: PAGOH, June 27 — Barisan Nasional (BN) has kicked off its campaign for the 16th Johor state election with high confidence in its bid to wrest back the Bukit Kepong seat from Perikatan Nasional (PN). Bukit Kepong, which falls under the Pagoh parliamentary constituency, has 37,683 eligible voters for this state election. The Johor state election will take place on July 11, with early voting on July 7.

items.title: Anwar announces RM1.5m allocation for Syariah legal aid, says reforms will not affect non-Muslims’ rights
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has announced a RM1.5 million allocation to strengthen access to Syariah legal aid services for the underprivileged, alongside renewed assurances that efforts to enhance the Syariah judicial system will not affect the rights of non-Muslims. He said the allocation to the Syariah Lawyers Association of Malaysia would be channelled through the Syariah Legal Aid Centre (BAGUS) to expand access to representation for those unable to afford Syariah legal services, particularly the poor and single mothers. “We have good laws, good courts, good lawyers and good judges, but access remains an issue.

items.title: In Malaysia, one in four rehab patients now battling addiction and mental illness
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS, June 27 — More than one in four drug addicts undergoing rehabilitation in Malaysia this year have also been diagnosed with mental health disorders, as the rise of synthetic drugs reshapes the country's addiction crisis. National Anti-Drug Agency (AADK) director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said 1,346 people, or 27 per cent of the 5,030 addicts and substance abusers undergoing treatment at Narcotics Addiction Rehabilitation Centres between January and March this year were found to have what is known as a dual diagnosis, the New Straits Times reported today. Ruslin said the trend showed drug rehabilitation was no longer just about treating addiction, as an increasing number of patients were also struggling with mental illness.

items.title: PH to roll out manifesto focused on federal alignment, development
items.summary: KULAI: Pakatan Harapan will soon unveil its Johor election manifesto, focusing on ensuring the state is governed by a coalition aligned with the federal government to secure greater development and benefits. PH secretariat chief Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the draft manifesto had been finalised but remained subject to final endorsement during a meeting with the coalition's top leadership this Monday. He said PH would emphasise performance-based politics over identity politics, which he described as divisive.

items.title: Anwar to launch LRT3 Shah Alam Line tomorrow
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) Shah Alam Line, spanning 37.8 kilometres from Bandar Utama to Johan Setia, has been fully completed and is ready for passenger service. "The new stations are Bandar Utama, Kayu Ara, BU 11 (Bandar Utama 11), Damansara Idaman, Subang, Glenmarie 2, Kerjaya, Stadium Shah Alam, Dato' Menteri and UiTM Shah Alam. "The remaining 10 stations are Seksyen 7 Shah Alam, Bandar Baru Klang, Pasar Klang, Jalan Meru, Jambatan Kota, Taman Selatan, Seri Andalas, Klang Jaya, Bandar Bukit Tinggi and Johan Setia," he told reporters during a pre-opening media ride on the new service.

items.title: Five-cornered fights set to shake up Bukit Batu, Puteri Wangsa, Bukit Naning
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Three key Johor state seats are set for five-cornered contests, setting the stage for unpredictable outcomes in Bukit Batu, Puteri Wangsa and Bukit Naning as rival blocs vie for fragmented voter support. In Bukit Batu, incumbent Pakatan Harapan assemblyman Arthur Chiong Sen Sern will defend his seat against Barisan Nasional's R. Kumaran, Muda's M. Premanand, Bersama's G. Tamili and independent Datuk Kamaruzaman Ali. In Puteri Wangsa, PH's Dr Maszlee Malik will face BN's Teow Chia Ling, Muda's Rashifa Aljunied, Bersama's Nicholas Paul Vincent and independent Wang Wee Siong.

items.title: Malaysia's biggest tech challenge is talent, not infrastructure, says Mosti
items.summary: Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation’s (MOSTI) deputy secretary-general (Planning and Science Enculturation) Datuk Ruziah Shafie said that developing a stronger, more resilient, and globally competitive future workforce requires early exposure to science, technology, and innovation (STI), as well as science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM), through continuous science culture and outreach programmes. She said that the National Science Week (MSN) programme is one of MOSTI’s key initiatives to cultivate youth’s interest in these fields while also bridging the gap between classroom learning and the real needs of high-technology industries. Malaysia can no longer remain merely a consumer of technology; we must rise to become a creator of technology.

items.title: 9 arrested in illegal bauxite mining raid, RM3.8mil haul seized in Kuantan
items.summary: KUANTAN: Authorities arrested nine people and seized about 10,000 tonnes of bauxite-bearing soil, heavy machinery and lorries worth a combined RM3.75 million in a raid on an illegal mining operation at a Felda plantation in Bukit Goh. According to Harian Metro, the raid at 11.30am on June 25 under Op Bersepadu Khazanah involved the General Operations Force (GOF) Battalion 7 Intelligence Branch and the State Enforcement Unit. Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure included soil suspected to contain bauxite worth RM2 million, two excavators valued at RM1 million and three lorries worth RM750,000.

items.title: Police tracking individual believed to have been with missing Nor Afiza
items.summary: TAIPING: Police are tracking an individual believed to have been with 41-year-old Nor Afiza Zainuddin before her disappearance 11 days ago. Acting Taiping police chief Superintendent Mohd Ariffridzuan Ezahar said the search for the mother of two was ongoing, with investigators pursuing fresh leads. Nor Afiza was reported missing on June 18.

items.title: Prescription for trouble: Unlicensed doctor at Subang Bestari clinic nabbed after eight months of cut-price consultations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — A Pakistani man suspected of posing as an unlicensed doctor has been detained after Immigration officers raided a private clinic in Subang Bestari, Selangor, uncovering what authorities believe was an illegally operated medical practice that had been running for eight months. The suspect is believed to have been working as an “unlicensed doctor” despite not possessing any valid pass or travel documents to remain in Malaysia, the Immigration Department said on Facebook today. The private clinic was allegedly run by a foreign national who did not have legal authorisation to work in Malaysia’s medical sector, the department said.

items.title: Johor polls: Maszlee pledges balanced development across all districts
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Puteri Wangsa state constituency, Dr Maszlee Malik, is determined to bring balanced development across all districts in Johor if given the mandate in the upcoming polls. He said a PH victory in Puteri Wangsa, and across the state, is crucial to usher in a new chapter of development that is not concentrated solely in southern Johor, but spread evenly so that every segment of society can enjoy a better quality of life. “We want Johor to progress in a balanced way across all its districts, not just the southern part.

items.title: Johor polls: Zahid pledges ‘mature politics’, says BN won’t attack rivals
items.summary: PONTIAN, June 27 — BN will adopt a ‘mature politics’ approach by refraining from attacking, criticising or disparaging its rivals throughout the campaign for the 16th Johor state election. BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the approach is important to preserve political harmony, while allowing the coalition’s campaign machinery to focus on promoting the strengths of its candidates courteously and constructively. In the 2022 Johor state election, BN secured a two-thirds majority after winning 40 of the 56 DUN seats to form the state government.

items.title: PM: Malaysia must accelerate adoption of drone technology
items.summary: SEPANG: Malaysia must accelerate its adoption and development of new technologies, particularly in the Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) or drone industry, to bolster its global economic standing, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the drone industry, which intersects with artificial intelligence (AI) and digital transformation, has emerged as a powerful catalyst for innovation, productivity and economic growth. Co-organised by the World UAV Federation (WUAVF) Malaysia Chapter and the Malaysia UAV Development Association (MUDA), the three-day expo surpassed expectations by featuring more than 100 exhibition booths and attracting thousands of visitors and industry delegates from 46 countries.

items.title: Police net two suspects, seize suspected heroin after Jalan Kuching pursuit that led to pile-up
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — Two men suspected of transporting heroin were arrested after a high-speed police chase ended in a multi-vehicle collision on Jalan Kuching, with officers recovering nearly 20g of suspected drugs from their car. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the suspects, aged 40 and 42, have been remanded to assist investigations, the New Straits Times reported this afternoon. Fadil said police instructed the driver to stop, but the vehicle instead sped off, triggering a pursuit towards Jalan Kuching.

items.title: Media Prima recognised for role in nationwide anti-drug awareness
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: Media Prima Bhd was today recognised by the National Anti-Drug Agency (Nada) for playing a key role in nationwide anti-drug awareness campaigns. Media Prima was also appointed Nada's strategic media partner, becoming the first media company to hold the role. Nada director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said Media Prima's extensive reach across television, radio, print and digital platforms made it an ideal partner in strengthening drug prevention efforts.

items.title: Johor polls: 172 candidates confirmed after nomination closes
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — The Election Commission (EC) has confirmed that 172 candidates from various political parties will contest the 16th Johor state election after the nomination process concluded at all 56 nomination centres today. Barisan Nasional (BN) and Pakatan Harapan (PH) are fielding 56 candidates each, followed by Perikatan Nasional (PN) with 33, Parti Bersama Malaysia with 15, the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (MUDA) with four, Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (Asli) and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) with one each, while six candidates are contesting as Independents. Ramlan said 14 seats would see straight fights, with 27 featuring three-cornered contests, 12 involving four candidates and three drawing five candidates.

items.title: Zahid does not rule out 'lower-level leader' talks between BN, Pas and Wawasan
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM: Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has not ruled out the possibility of internal discussions on cooperation involving Pas and Parti Wawasan Negara (Wawasan) in the Johor election. However, he said such discussions, if they had taken place, were likely held at lower levels within the party. Zahid, who is also Deputy Prime Minister, said BN remained focused on contesting all 56 seats under its own banner.

items.title: [UPDATED] Elevating Syariah court status won't affect non-Muslims' rights, says PM
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Efforts to strengthen the Syariah court system will not infringe on the rights of non-Muslims to freely practise their religion, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar said the government remained committed to enhancing the status of the Syariah courts while safeguarding the rights of non-Muslims and addressing their concerns. Anwar said Putrajaya was actively addressing structural and systemic gaps in the Syariah justice system.

items.title: BN eyes higher seat count in Johor election, says Zahid
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM: Barisan Nasional (BN) is targeting an increase in the number of seats in the Johor election compared with the previous polls, its chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. Zahid, who is also Umno president, however, stopped short of revealing BN's target for the July 11 election. In the previous state election, BN secured a two-thirds majority, winning 40 of the 56 state seats.

items.title: Pakistani man held for allegedly posing as doctor in Selangor clinic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department has detained a Pakistani man suspected of posing as a doctor at a private clinic in Subang Bestari, Selangor, in a crackdown on illegal medical practice involving foreigners. "The clinic is also believed to have been operated by a foreign national without authorisation to work in Malaysia's medical sector. The department also believes the clinic issued medical certificates without conducting any medical examination or health assessment, charging RM20 for each certificate.

items.title: In Pagoh, Muhyiddin shrugs off PAS snub, vows Bersatu machinery will drive Johor fight
items.summary: PAGOH, June 27 — Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) founding president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin said he respected PAS’ decision not to assist the party’s campaign in the 16th Johor state election, stressing that Bersatu would press ahead on the strength of its own machinery. He said he believed the decision would not affect Bersatu’s campaign, which remained focused on the party’s policies and track record in winning public support. Muhyiddin said Bersatu would nevertheless remain open to assisting its coalition partners if its support was needed during the Johor election campaign.

items.title: Muhyiddin confident PN has chance to form Johor government despite contesting 33 seats
items.summary: PAGOH: Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin is confident that there is still a chance for Perikatan Nasional (PN) to form the next state government despite the opposition coalition contesting only 33 seats in the Johor election. There is still a chance (for PN to form the next state government). PN needs to secure 29 seats to achieve a simple majority to form the next state government.

items.title: Same village, different flags: Harmonious contest expected in Semerah
items.summary: "We're all from Semerah, from the same village," said Barisan Nasional candidate Mohd Fared Khalid. Fared will defend the seat against Pakatan Harapan's Khuzzan Abu Bakar and Perikatan Nasional's Halim Kepol. Fared said he would rely on his track record of service in seeking another term.

items.title: Drug suspects arrested after high-speed chase, crash in KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two suspects allegedly transporting drugs in a Perodua Viva led police on a high-speed chase before crashing into three other vehicles in a bid to escape along Jalan Kuching here yesterday. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said both suspects were remanded today to assist investigations. Lee traffic police station at 03-2071 9999, or any nearby police station.

items.title: Youths make up 84pct of synthetic drug users
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: Youths aged between 15 and 39 account for 84 per cent of Malaysia's synthetic drug users, with the highest abuse rates recorded in east coast states. National Anti-Drug Agency director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said the trend reflected the growing dominance of synthetic drugs, which now accounted for 73 per cent of all drug abuse cases nationwide. Ruslin said east coast states continued to record the highest rates of synthetic drug abuse, particularly involving yaba pills and syabu, although cases were also rising in west coast states.

items.title: Indonesian police arrest dozens in anti-government protest [WATCH]
items.summary: SURABAYA: Indonesian police have arrested dozens of protesters following a rally against President Prabowo Subianto's policies in the country's second-largest city, a rights group said on Saturday. Around 100 people gathered near a government building in Surabaya on Friday, AFP journalists estimated, to protest a fuel price hike and Prabowo's flagship free meals scheme. Twenty-four protesters were arrested, Fatkul Khoir, a coordinator at rights group KontraS Surabaya, told AFP on Saturday.

items.title: PM Anwar to launch LRT3 Shah Alam Line tomorrow, 20 new stations set to transform commutes
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 27 — The Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) Shah Alam Line, spanning 37.8 kilometres from Bandar Utama to Johan Setia, has been fully completed and is now ready for passenger service. “The new stations are Bandar Utama, Kayu Ara, BU 11 (Bandar Utama 11), Damansara Idaman, Subang, Glenmarie 2, Kerjaya, Stadium Shah Alam, Dato’ Menteri, and UiTM Shah Alam. “The remaining 10 stations are Seksyen 7 Shah Alam, Bandar Baru Klang, Pasar Klang, Jalan Meru, Jambatan Kota, Taman Selatan, Seri Andalas, Klang Jaya, Bandar Bukit Tinggi, and Johan Setia,” he told reporters during a recent pre-opening media ride on the new service.

items.title: Johor election: Maszlee says PH win will usher in new chapter for Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A win for Pakatan Harapan at Puteri Wangsa and other seats in the Johor election will usher in a new chapter for the development of the state, former education minister Dr Maszlee Malik said. "Victory in Puteri Wangsa and across the state will usher in a new chapter for Johor," he told reporters after the nomination process for the seat concluded today. Maszlee said he wants to introduce balanced development and progress in the state constituency in the event he wins the election.

items.title: Synthetic drug abuse fuels rise in mental health disorders among addicts
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: Synthetic drug abuse is fuelling a sharp rise in mental health disorders among addicts, posing a growing challenge to rehabilitation efforts. National Anti-Drug Agency director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said the agency was seeing an alarming increase in "dual diagnosis" cases, where addicts were also diagnosed with psychiatric disorders. "From January to March this year, 1,346 people, or 27 per cent of the 5,030 drug addicts and substance abusers undergoing institutional treatment and rehabilitation programmes at Narcotics Addiction Rehabilitation Centres, were found to have dual diagnosis."

items.title: Tuanku Muhriz installs Muhammad Faris as 11th Undang of Luak Sungei Ujong, calls for loyalty
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, June 27 — The Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, has formalised the installation of Muhammad Faris Johari as the 11th Undang of Luak Sungei Ujong at Istana Besar Seri Menanti this morning. Tuanku Muhriz then formally proclaimed Muhammad Faris as the 11th Undang of Luak Sungei Ujong, conferring on him the title Datuk Klana Petra. Also in attendance were Tunku Ampuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Aishah Rohani Tengku Besar Mahmud, Tunku Besar Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin Tuanku Muhriz, and Tunku Panglima Besar Tunku Zain Al-‘Abidin.

items.title: PH faces four-cornered fight to retain Skudai
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The battle for Skudai has developed into a four-cornered contest involving Barisan Nasional, Pakatan Harapan, Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) candidates. The seat will see a four-way fight involving BN's Nicol Tan, PH's J. Kartiyaini, Bersama's Eugene Chua and PSM's Amir Syafiq Ameer Soekre. Nicol, 43, said restoring dignity to residents through better housing would be her first priority if elected.

items.title: Malaysia to seize drone boom, PM Anwar promises stronger rules, research and industry collaboration
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today pledged government backing to build Malaysia’s unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) industry into a new engine of economic growth, saying Putrajaya will strengthen regulations, research and collaboration to accelerate the sector’s development. Speaking at the closing ceremony of the country’s first drone exhibition today, Anwar said the government has instructed ministries, research institutions and relevant agencies to provide the support needed to develop the local UAV ecosystem as drone technology becomes increasingly important for both commercial and defence applications. Citing the growing adoption of drones alongside artificial intelligence and digital technologies, Anwar said Malaysia must move quickly to seize opportunities in what is expected to become a rapidly expanding global market.

items.title: Bukit Batu contest heats up, five candidates challenge Pakatan incumbent in high‑stakes battle
items.summary: KULAI, June 27 — The Bukit Batu state constituency is set to see a five-way contest, turning the once quiet seat into a ‘hot seat’ for the upcoming Johor state election. The contest for Bukit Batu will see incumbent Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Arthur Chiong Sen Sern defend his seat from Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate RK Kumaran; Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) candidate, M. Premanand; Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) candidate, G. Tamili and independent hopeful Datuk Kamaruzaman Ali. In the last state election in 2022, Chiong won by a razor-thin majority of 137 votes with a total of 9,439 votes in a four-cornered contest for the Bukit Batu state seat.

items.title: Foreigner who threw brick at police car remanded for 14 days
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A foreigner who threw a brick at a police car in front of a hotel in Jalan Hang Lekiu has been remanded for another 14 days. Dang Wangi deputy police chief Superintendent Nuzulan Mohd Din said the suspect, a vagrant in his 40s, is being remanded to facilitate investigations under the Immigration Act. It was reported yesterday that the suspect, who is believed to have health issues, was arrested after damaging a police car in front of the hotel.

items.title: Public backs Penang's community service order for litterbugs
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang's community service order for litterbugs has received strong public support ahead of its July 1 enforcement, with residents, traders and tourists backing the measure. State Local Government and Town and Country Planning Committee chairman H'ng Mooi Lye said feedback gathered during a public awareness campaign in Lebuh Armenian showed broad support for the initiative. H'ng said the state government hoped the initiative would encourage the public to take greater responsibility for keeping public spaces clean and help maintain Penang as a clean, attractive and comfortable destination.

items.title: Johor election: Muhyiddin says 'it's alright' after Pas machinery snub
items.summary: PAGOH: Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin appeared unfazed by Pas' decision not to deploy its election machinery at seats contested by Bersatu candidates in the state election. The Bersatu president, who is also Pagoh member of parliament, said he accepted the decision made by Pas despite the two parties contesting under Perikatan Nasional in the July 11 polls. If anyone wants to help, then help.

items.title: Heritage festival spotlights revival of KL Railway Station
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Railway Assets Corporation (RAC) is holding The Heritage Colours Festival at the Kuala Lumpur Railway Station from June 26-28 as part of efforts to promote appreciation of one of Malaysia's best-known heritage buildings and encourage greater public engagement with the site. The festival's opening day also featured the second edition of Jelajah Warisan KL, a stakeholder engagement programme that reviewed ongoing conservation and activation efforts around the Kuala Lumpur Railway Station heritage precinct. The programme, held under the Warisan KL initiative, was officiated by Kuala Lumpur mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud and attended by senior representatives from RAC, the Warisan KL secretariat, Jabatan Warisan Negara, Keretapi Tanah Melayu Bhd, Kuala Lumpur City Hall, Think City and local community groups.

items.title: Police arrest man over assault on food delivery rider
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Police have arrested a man over the assault of a food delivery rider during a delivery in Kelana Jaya yesterday. Petaling Jaya deputy district police chief Superintendent M. Hussin Sollehuddin Zolkifly said the suspect, in his 30s, was detained to assist in the investigation. Recounting the incident, Man said he accepted an order from a fast-food restaurant at a petrol station in SS6, Kelana Jaya, about 4.45am for delivery to Taman Rasa Sayang.

items.title: Faris Johari accepted as 11th Undang Luak Sungei Ujong
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir has accepted the proclamation of Muhammad Faris Johari as the 11th Undang Luak Sungei Ujong. The proclamation took place during a ceremony at Istana Besar Seri Menanti, where Tuanku Muhriz granted an audience to Muhammad Faris, who was selected in accordance with Sungei Ujong's customary laws. "I am pleased to accept the proclamation of Muhammad Faris Johari as the 11th Undang Luak Sungei Ujong with the title Dato Klana Petra," Tuanku Muhriz said in his royal address.

items.title: Bloomberg Malaysia strike-out bid rejected
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has dismissed Bloomberg (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd's application to strike out a libel suit filed by Bestinet Sdn Bhd and its founder Datuk Seri Aminul Islam Abdul Nor over reports alleging migrant worker exploitation involving the company. The judge said among the pleadings which need further examination were whether Bloomberg (Malaysia) was indeed involved in the actual publication of the disputed article, or if it has control over or operates the main Bloomberg website. The Bloomberg website, he argued, clearly states that Bloomberg LP - the parent company based in New York, US - operates the website and had published the article.

items.title: Johor polls see 172 candidates, 42 multi-cornered contests
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: A total of 172 candidates will contest the 56 seats in the Johor election, with 42 constituencies set to witness multi-cornered contests. In terms of age, 50 candidates are aged 40-49, followed by 48 aged 50-59, 38 aged 30-39, 27 aged 60 and above, and nine aged between 21 and 29. To ensure compliance with election laws, Ramlan said the EC had established 56 Election Campaign Enforcement Teams to monitor campaign activities throughout the campaign period.

items.title: Drug syndicates exploiting encrypted platforms, digital black markets, says Saifuddin
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: International drug syndicates are exploiting encrypted communication platforms, digital black markets and cross-border financial transactions to expand their trafficking networks, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said. He said advances in digital technology had made the drug crime ecosystem increasingly complex and harder to detect. "The evolution of digital technology has been manipulated by international syndicates to expand drug trafficking networks through encrypted communication platforms, digital black markets and cross-border financial transactions.

items.title: Synthetic drugs dominate drug abuse cases, says Saifuddin
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: Synthetic drugs now account for nearly three-quarters of drug abuse cases in Malaysia, prompting authorities to step up artificial intelligence-driven enforcement and prevention efforts. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said amphetamine-type stimulants (ATS), including methamphetamine (syabu), ecstasy and yaba pills, continued to dominate drug abuse trends nationwide. Saifuddin said drug abuse was no longer confined to urban areas but had spread to rural communities, educational institutions and workplaces.

items.title: King urges modernisation of Malaysian Armed Forces with technology-driven defence assets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 27 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today called for a strategic transformation of the Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) in line with the ‘Future Force’ concept to build an integrated military capable of meeting the challenges of fifth-generation warfare. “I therefore call on the government to prioritise the acquisition of modern, technology-driven defence assets and support the new structure of the Malaysian Armed Forces,” His Majesty said. Sultan Ibrahim said this at the Royal Commissioning Parade and Presentation of Commissioning Scrolls to Malaysian Armed Forces Officer Cadets 2026 at Dataran Panglima Tentera Darat, Kem Perdana Sungai Besi, here today.—MORE Also present were Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin and Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) chief Gen Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman.

items.title: Former health minister Dr Adham Baba makes political comeback in three-way Pasir Raja contest
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 27 — Former Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba will make his electoral comeback in a three-cornered contest for the Pasir Raja seat in the 16th Johor state election. Polling for the Johor state election will be held on July 11, with early voting scheduled for July 7. For the latest updates on the Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/.

items.title: MOH website hacked, ministry warns Malaysians avoid site and rely on verified platforms
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 27 — Malaysia’s Health Ministry has confirmed its official website was hacked after the portal displayed a message believed to have been left by the attackers. The incident forced the site offline and sparked urgent recovery efforts. “The ministry takes this incident seriously and is working closely with relevant agencies to strengthen system security and prevent a recurrence,” it said.

items.title: Police: Johor nomination day concluded peacefully with 4,832 personnel deployed
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — The nomination process for the 16th Johor state election at all 56 nomination centres proceeded smoothly today without any untoward incidents. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said police had not received any reports of incidents that could disrupt the process. He said 4,832 police officers and personnel were deployed to ensure the nomination process ran smoothly.

items.title: Two-term incumbent Ee Chin Li faces BN challenger How Chin Teck in Tangkak race
items.summary: TANGKAK, June 27 — The Tangkak state constituency will see a straight fight between candidates from Barisan Nasional (BN) and Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the Johor state election, scheduled for July 11. The contest was announced by Tangkak election returning officer Ungku Suzana Ungku Mohammad at the nomination centre, Dewan Jubli Intan Sultan Ibrahim here, after the nomination process closed at 10 am. The Election Commission (EC) has set polling day for July 11, with early voting scheduled for July 7.

items.title: Onn Hafiz says ‘anything can happen’ in Machap straight fight, vows to fight hard
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM, June 27 — Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate for the Machap state seat, Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, has said that anything can happen in the Johor state election despite the contest being a straight fight. “In an election, anything can happen. In this Johor state election, Onn Hafiz will face Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Nor Hafiz Roslan.

items.title: Machap to see two-way battle as BN's Onn Hafiz takes on Pakatan's Nor Hafiz
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM, June 27 — Barisan Nasional's Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi is defending the Machap seat in a straight fight with Nor Hafiz Roslan of Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the 16th Johor state election. Nor Hafiz submitted his nomination papers at 9.03 am, while Onn Hafiz, the Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate, submitted his at 9.06 am. In the previous state election, Onn Hafiz retained the seat with a majority of 6,543 votes, defeating Perikatan Nasional candidate Azlisham Azahar, Malaysian United Democratic Alliance candidate R. Sangaran and Parti Pejuang Tanah Air candidate Sharuddin Md Salleh.

items.title: Semerah sees three-way contest as BN, Pakatan and Perikatan candidates face off
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 27 — Pakatan Harapan (PH), Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) are set to go head-to-head for the Semerah state seat in the 16th Johor state election. The Election Commission (EC) has set polling day for July 11, following the dissolution of the state assembly on June 1. For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, please visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/.

items.title: Johor royal family’s presence in political dialogue highlights commitment to prioritising Johoreans’ welfare
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, June 27 — The Johor royal family has been described as a vital bridge for political dialogue, with Regent of Johor Tunku Mahkota Ismail Sultan Ibrahim frequently meeting leaders from across the political spectrum. In a post yesterday, Fans of TMJ said the Johor royals, particularly Tunku Mahkota Ismail, often hold discussions with figures from various parties and ministries. The approach, it added, reflects the royal family’s commitment to ensuring issues affecting Johoreans are not overlooked despite political differences.

items.title: Johor nomination closes as 14-day election campaign officially begins
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — The nomination process for the 16th Johor State Election concluded at 10am today following the closure of all 56 nomination centres (PPC) statewide. The one-hour window for submission of nomination papers ran from 9am, with returning officers (RO) announcing the closure at their respective centres. At several nomination centres, supporters gathered and accompanied candidates from their respective parties to the venues.

items.title: Food rider says punched in face while delivering order, Petaling Jaya police investigating assault
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 27 — A 28 year old food delivery rider, who asked to be identified only as Man (not his real name), was injured after being assaulted by a customer in Kelana Jaya yesterday. Man had collected an order from a fast food outlet at a petrol station in Kelana Jaya at about 4.45am for delivery to a premises in Taman Rasa Sayang. Man displayed the QR code for payment, but before any transaction was made another man moved behind his motorcycle.

items.title: Johor polls: Onn Hafiz files nomination for Machap with Zahid, Hishammuddin and Khairy by his side
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM, June 27 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi submitted his nomination papers for the Johor state election at 9.10 am at the nomination centre at the Muafakat Hall of the Simpang Renggam District Council. The incumbent Machap state assemblyman was accompanied by Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Sembrong MP Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Tun Hussein and former minister Khairy Jamaluddin. Also present to lend support was former Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad.

items.title: Two burnt to death, one survives after car skids and burns at DUKE exit near Jalan Kuching
items.summary: The Kuala Lumpur Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Centre, in a statement said it received an emergency call at 5.20am. Firefighters from the Jinjang and Sri Hartamas Fire and Rescue Stations were deployed to the scene for rescue operations. Upon arrival, the team found a vehicle engulfed in flames with three victims inside.

items.title: Mohamad Sabu leads Pakatan supporters as Sharon Teo enters Permas nomination race
items.summary: PASIR GUDANG, June 27 — Chants of “Johor undi Pakatan Harapan” (Johor votes for Pakatan Harapan) rang out as Sharon Teo made her way to the Permas state constituency nomination centre at Dewan Muafakat Taman Mawar here, marking the start of the state election. Also seen marching to the nomination centre was Permas incumbent and Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Baharudin Mohamed Taib, accompanied by hundreds of supporters at 8.35 am. For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, please visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/.

items.title: Budi Diesel expanded to cover company-owned pick-ups and jeeps as subsidy system gets tweaks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 27 — The Madani Government has announced three enhancements to the implementation of the targeted Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) subsidy system to facilitate the transition ahead of the programme’s early access phase, which begins tomorrow in Peninsular Malaysia. The enhancements are the implementation of the Temporary Incentive Programme for the Exemption of Vehicle Ownership Transfer (THM) Fees for diesel vehicles for three months beginning July 1, allowing subsidy eligibility to be transferred to the actual users of vehicles, and the expansion of BUDI Diesel to cover company-owned private pick-up trucks and jeeps. At the same time, more private diesel vehicle owners in Peninsular Malaysia who had previously not received Budi Diesel Individu cash assistance will gain access to subsidised diesel through Budi Diesel.

items.title: Kedah Immigration detains second suspect linked to migrant smuggling at Bukit Kayu Hitam
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 27 — The Kedah Immigration Department (JIM) has detained another man suspected of involvement in migrant smuggling during Ops Serkap at the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex on Thursday. It said the 36-year-old suspect was detained by the Intelligence and Special Operations Division of the department at the complex while he was present for immigration-related travel arrangements. The court has granted a remand order for four days until June 29 to assist investigations, it said.
